lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: Good day, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: I am Lord D'rows, leader of the Oathbreakers. Yourself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm [player name]. That sounds pretty important.

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: It is a job I do not take lightly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's this oath that you're all so proud of breaking?

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: Pride? There is no pride in what we've done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which was?

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: We Oathbreakers were all once monks of Saradomin but have since seen that his light is false. The founder of this sanctuary had a similar story and that is why we follow in her footsteps.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What caused your faith in Saradomin to wane?

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: How can order and wisdom prevail in a land where chaos and destruction reign supreme? That, and a few too many muggings during my travels. If you can't beat them, become them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: Was there anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you here?

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: We've been keeping an eye on Slepe lately. We could sense a darkness building as the citizens fell into their endless slumber.

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: Once we'd heard that the sanctuary had been broken into, we came here posthaste.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Broken into?

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: Indeed. This place was sealed, only to be reopened once Mother had awoken from her deep sleep.

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: This nightmarish creature that the siren warned us of has made a home here. That was reason enough for her to unseal the entrance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, um, whose mother? Yours?

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: Oh, not my actual mother, you fool. Phosani, the founder of this sanctuary went by the title of 'Mother' while her followers adopted the title of 'Sister'. I'm sure you've seen them around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have. I suspect you're quite bothered about the sudden influx of visitors?

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: Well, yes, but that fish woman claims every warrior is needed to defeat the Nightmare and that everyone asleep here is at risk.

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: We must be strong, adventurer. Be strong for Mother.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... Does this creature have treasure? That's kind of a sticking point for me.

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: Treasure? This sanctuary is full of old trinkets which the hag has claimed as her own. Kill her and take them, as is the Zamorakian way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, the iron price. My specialty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your friends are a quiet bunch.

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: They took a vow of silence. I speak for them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, how come?

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: It is the way. When I fall, one of them will take my place as the Oath Lord.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No disagreements, that must be nice.

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: I never claimed that they can't communicate. They know a rudimentary hand-talk to vent their frustrations with my leadership. Making decisions is easier when no one can shout at you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh, I can imagine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bye!

lines.speaker: Oath Lord D'rows
lines.text: Stay strong, adventurer.
